description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages build, push and deploy using the docker:dind service. In the build stage add a build_app job that runs on the docker:stable-git image, executes before_script "docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_JOB_TOKEN registry.gitlab.com", caches the file image.tar with push policy, and runs only on master, develop, cloud or lite branches; its script echoes "Building image...", sets DEV=$(echo $GITLAB_USER_NAME | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]'), pulls $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/dev:$DEV if it exists, builds the image with "docker build --rm -f docker/Dockerfile --cache-from ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/dev:$DEV -t $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE -t $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/dev --build-arg BUILD=$CI_PIPELINE_IID --build-arg BUILD_DATE=\"$(date --utc +%FT%TZ)\" --build-arg METEOR_SETTINGS=\"$(cat src/tools/settings.json)\" --build-arg VCS_REF=\"$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)\" --build-arg RELEASE=$APP_RELEASE --build-arg VERSION=$APP_VERSION .", saves it with "docker save $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE -o image.tar", tags it as "$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_PIPELINE_IID" and "$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/dev:$DEV", and pushes the dev tag. In the deploy stage add deploy_app (production), deploy_dev (dev), deploy_new (staging) and deploy_infrastructure jobs all using the antmounds/cd-builder image and docker:dind service; each runs terraform init, plan and apply with appropriate targets (aws_ecs_service.app_service_hot, app_service_dev, app_service_warm or generic) and passes the pipeline ID as a variable (app_version_hot, app_version_dev, app_version_warm), with deploy_app, deploy_dev and deploy_new limited to the cloud or develop branches and set to manual, while deploy_infrastructure triggers only on changes in the infrastructure/ directory and also runs manually with AWS credentials. In the push stage add push_aws, push_dockerhub, push_gitlab and push_quay jobs that each load image.tar (pull cache), log in to their respective registries in before_script, tag the built image for AWS ECR, Docker Hub, GitLab registry or Quay.io using commands like "docker tag $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE $AWS_IMAGE_URI" and "docker tag $DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME $DOCKERHUB_IMAGE_NAME:$APP_IMAGE_VERSION", then push the tags with "docker push"; these jobs cache image.tar with pull policy, run only on specified branches (master, develop, cloud, lite or lite), and use environment variables for AWS, Docker Hub and Quay credentials.
